# RCX-Registry — full reference

> An MCP-compatible subregistry serving a mirror of more than 17,000 servers from the official Model
> Context Protocol registry. The repository publishes a frozen Ed25519 receipt format and byte-exact
> conformance vectors. Hosted production has zero snapshots because Vault Transit returns 403, and
> all public publisher verification/declaration writes return 404. Snapshot and enrichment histories
> use distinct, spec-defined link targets rather than one universal chain.
>
> Frontdoor: https://rcxprotocol.org · API: https://registry.rcxprotocol.org ·
> Source (Apache-2.0): https://github.com/CueCrux/RCX-Registry
> Independent project by CueCrux Limited. Not affiliated with Anthropic or the MCP project.

## Why it exists

MCP registries verify who a publisher is at publish time. Nothing about that makes the registry's
history tamper-evident, and nothing catches a server that turns malicious after approval. The last
year of MCP supply-chain incidents is that gap repeating:

- postmark-mcp (Sept 2025): an npm-published MCP server ran clean for 15 versions, then shipped an
  update that BCC'd every email it handled to the author. ~300 organisations were affected. A
  rug-pull that per-version receipts and history diffing would surface.
- CVE-2025-54136 ("MCP rug pull"): a tool definition mutated after a client had already approved it.
- CVE-2025-6514: command injection in mcp-remote, a connector with ~437,000 downloads.
- MCPTox: adversarial instructions hidden in tool descriptions reached a 72.8% success rate.
- Microsoft advisory (June 2026): an estimated ~200,000 vulnerable MCP instances in the wild.

RCX-Registry's goal is the part the others skip: signed snapshot and background-enrichment evidence,
attributable namespace rights, and byte-exact formats with type-specific history links. Today the
reproducible deliverables are the format, source, and vectors. Production signing recovery, complete
artifact persistence, proof binding, retrieval, and key discovery remain open.

## How it works

1. Mirror — the implementation can walk every upstream page and mint a RegistrySnapshot receipt over
   a flat, sorted BLAKE3 digest. Hosted production serves the mirrored read dataset, but its sync
   attempt currently fails at Vault signing before snapshot persistence. The historical wire field remains
   `snapshot_merkle_root`, but v1 defines no Merkle tree or inclusion proof. Upstream deletions are
   soft-deleted with a 30-day retention window.
2. Verify — DNS, GitHub, and manual proof routes all return 404 at the production edge pending trust hardening.
3. Enrich — the background implementation can validate, hash, sign, and refresh declarations, but
   public declaration submission is disabled and storage retains hash references rather than full artifacts.
4. Receipt — the format pins Ed25519 preimages and test signatures. Hosted live signing, full artifact
   persistence, public retrieval, and production-key discovery remain explicit M1a work.

## API surface

Baseline mirror (shape-compatible with the upstream MCP registry), served from
https://registry.rcxprotocol.org:

- GET /v0/servers — list servers, cursor pagination, `limit` parameter.
- GET /v0/servers/{name}/versions — list versions for a server.
- GET /v0/servers/{name}/versions/{version} — fetch one version.
- GET https://rcxprotocol.org/openapi.json — self-contained OpenAPI spec for the registry API (inline definitions, no external $ref).

Publisher rights + enrichment (RCX extensions):

- GET /publish — publisher onboarding page.
- DNS challenge/verify, GitHub start/callback, manual review, and publisher declaration routes all
  return 404 at the production edge.
- GET /v0/publishers/{publisher_passport} — public rights read; production currently has zero rows.

## Verification model

- Canonical form + BLAKE3 hash per server envelope (two parties with the same data compute the same
  hash).
- A flat, sorted BLAKE3 stream commits to every server in a snapshot; the historical field name is
  `snapshot_merkle_root`, but it is not a Merkle tree and provides no inclusion proof.
- The format signs snapshot and background-enrichment receipts with Ed25519 over full canonical CBOR
  with only `receipt_signature` zeroed. Production is designed to use a Vault Transit key, but current
  calls return 403 and no snapshot has been persisted.
- Rights and enrichment storage paths retain deterministic receipt-hash references rather than
  complete signed artifacts. Public writes are currently closed.
- Snapshot history links `previous_snapshot_hash` to the prior snapshot set digest. Enrichment
  history links `supersedes_prior` to the prior enrichment `receipt_hash`. Other receipt types do
  not form one generic chain.
- Reproducible today: exact conformance vectors and the Apache-2.0 implementation. No live hosted
  signed snapshot exists; the public `/v0` API does not expose full receipts, and `signer_kid` does
  not discover a production public key.

## Protocol specification

The normative `rcx-spec/v1` wire-format freeze is published at
https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1 from repository commit
`310ace858172b6f4acdd982ef25c2441f20d6b7a`. It specifies canonical CBOR, canonical JSON, hashing
inputs, CROWN receipts, the snapshot-set digest and chaining, API version selection, and errors.
The conformance-vector index is https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1/vectors; each JSON fixture beneath
that URL is served byte-for-byte from `spec/v1/vectors/` so independent implementations can pin and
reproduce the exact corpus.

## Subregistry positioning

The MCP registry documents an open subregistry model: implement the read shape and add value through
custom `_meta` fields, no approval step. RCX-Registry is exactly that. Its entire footprint sits under
`_meta.org.rcxprotocol.registry`:

- `.../publisher` — publisher-derived: publisher metadata, verification state when available, and a
  deterministic BLAKE3 `enrichment_receipt_hash` reference.
- `.../auto` — registry-derived: upstream enrichment metadata and a deterministic BLAKE3
  `auto_enrichment_receipt` reference when the block is produced.

Pointing clients at it: any client with a configurable registry base URL points here by changing the
URL. VS Code's enterprise policy `McpGalleryServiceUrl` repoints its whole MCP gallery at any
spec-compliant endpoint — set it to https://registry.rcxprotocol.org.

## Pages

- https://rcxprotocol.org/ — home
- https://rcxprotocol.org/verify — verification model
- https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1 — normative Protocol Spec v1 + conformance vectors
- https://rcxprotocol.org/publish — publisher onboarding
- https://rcxprotocol.org/subregistry — subregistry positioning
- https://rcxprotocol.org/badge — README badge
- https://rcxprotocol.org/legal — terms and privacy
